text.title: Prompt Changelog Discipline — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules that gate any application of the prompt-changelog-discipline methodology. Sources: Anthropic prompt-engineering iteration chapter; Microsoft LMOps team writeups on prompt versioning; Vellum / Pezzo / Helicone prompt-management docs.

rule.statement: Each prompt lives in its own file (or its own keyed YAML entry); prompts MUST NOT be inlined into source as string literals.
rule.rationale: Inline prompts cannot be hashed or diffed cleanly; externalising is the prerequisite for any changelog discipline.

rule.statement: Pre-commit hook computes a stable content hash (SHA-256 truncated to 16 chars, whitespace-normalised) for every changed prompt and writes it into the changelog.

rationale.source: Microsoft LMOps team writeups on prompt versioning

rule.rationale: A stable hash lets observability platforms tag each LLM call with the exact prompt version, making regression triage minutes not days.

rule.statement: Every changelog entry has a one-sentence `intent` and an `eval_delta` field populated by the CI eval runner; entries missing either are blocked at review.
rule.rationale: Intent without eval is wishful thinking; eval without intent loses the why; the CI source prevents hand-typed score fudging.

rule.statement: Every entry records the immediately-previous prompt hash in `rollback_hash`, regardless of confidence.
rule.rationale: A regression at 3 AM is not the time to figure out the previous prompt; recorded rollback hash turns rollback into a one-liner.

rule.statement: Every production deploy ships a `deploy-summary.md` listing every prompt change with hash, eval delta, rollout strategy; deploys without it are blocked at the release gate.
rule.rationale: Without per-deploy summaries you cannot correlate a regression to a specific release.
